title: Bounded-Rational Usability Regression Testing: Reducing Cognitive-Model Composition to Constrained Inference over UI State Spaces
abstract: We present a formal framework for automated usability regression testing that reduces cognitive-model composition to constrained inference over UI state spaces. The key insight is that information-theoretic bounded rationality, specifically the free-energy formulation F(pi) = E_pi[R] - (1/beta) D_KL(pi || p_0), provides a unifying variational objective that recovers classical cognitive performance laws (Fitts' law, Hick-Hyman law, visual search, working memory decay) as capacity-constrained channels. By parsing UI accessibility trees into task-state Markov Decision Processes, annotating transitions with compositional cognitive costs, and computing bounded-rational policies, the system detects usability regressions with formal error bounds suitable for CI/CD integration. Three core theoretical contributions enable this: (1) bounded-rational bisimulation for tractable state-space reduction, (2) a compositional cognitive cost algebra with soundness guarantees, and (3) an information-theoretic bottleneck taxonomy for actionable diagnostics.

key_concepts.name: Free Energy
key_concepts.description: Variational objective F(pi) = E_pi[R] - (1/beta) D_KL(pi || p_0) trading reward against information-processing cost

key_concepts.name: Bounded Rationality
key_concepts.description: Information-processing capacity parameter controlling deviation from optimal behavior

key_concepts.name: Cognitive Cost Tuple
key_concepts.description: Mean cost, variance, and kurtosis characterizing cognitive effort distribution

key_concepts.name: Sequential Composition
key_concepts.description: Algebra operator for composing sequential cognitive operations

key_concepts.name: Parallel Interference
key_concepts.description: Algebra operator for modeling concurrent resource competition

key_concepts.name: Context Modulation
key_concepts.description: Algebra operator for prior-context effects on subsequent operations

key_concepts.name: Cognitive Distance
key_concepts.description: Behavioral pseudometric weighting state distinguishability by agent capacity

key_concepts.name: Bisimulation Quotient
key_concepts.description: Reduced MDP preserving cost orderings under bounded rationality

key_concepts.name: Channel Capacity
key_concepts.description: Information-theoretic capacity of cognitive subsystem i (bits/sec)

key_concepts.name: Regression Verdict
key_concepts.description: Classification: PASS, REGRESSION, IMPROVEMENT with confidence bounds

key_concepts.name: Bottleneck Type
key_concepts.description: Classification of failure mode by channel-capacity signature

key_concepts.name: Accessibility Tree
key_concepts.description: Structured representation of UI interactive elements and their properties

key_concepts.name: Task-State MDP
key_concepts.description: Markov Decision Process encoding UI navigation as state transitions

key_concepts.name: Softmax Policy
key_concepts.description: Bounded-rational policy derived from free-energy minimization

key_concepts.name: Mutual Information
key_concepts.description: Information transmitted through cognitive processing channel

key_concepts.name: Index of Difficulty
key_concepts.description: Fitts' law information-theoretic formulation of motor targeting difficulty

bounded_rationality.description: Framework based on Ortega & Braun (2013) information-theoretic bounded rationality
bounded_rationality.core_principle: Agents minimize free energy F(pi) = E_pi[R] - (1/beta) D_KL(pi || p_0), trading task reward against information-processing cost measured by KL divergence from a prior policy p_0
bounded_rationality.key_result: The optimal policy under bounded rationality is a softmax: pi*(a|s) = p_0(a|s) exp(beta Q*(s,a)) / Z(s), where Q* satisfies a modified Bellman equation
bounded_rationality.connection_to_cognitive_laws: Classical cognitive laws emerge as special cases: Fitts' law corresponds to motor channel capacity, Hick-Hyman to choice channel capacity, visual search to perceptual channel capacity

free_energy_formulation.description: Variational formulation unifying cognitive cost models
free_energy_formulation.interpretation: First term is expected task reward (negative cost), second term is information-processing cost (KL divergence from prior policy scaled by inverse capacity)
free_energy_formulation.subsystem_decomposition: For K cognitive subsystems with capacities beta_i: F(pi) = E_pi[R] - sum_{i=1}^{K} (1/beta_i) D_KL(pi_i || p_{0,i})
free_energy_formulation.connection_to_rate_distortion: The free-energy objective is dual to rate-distortion: minimizing distortion (task cost) subject to rate constraint (information processing capacity)

cognitive_cost_algebra.description: Compositional algebra for combining cognitive cost models

operators.sequential: c_1 oplus c_2 = (mu_1 + mu_2 + gamma(c_1, c_2), sigma_1^2 + sigma_2^2 + 2*Cov(c_1,c_2), ...)
operators.parallel: c_1 otimes c_2 = (max(mu_1, mu_2) + I(c_1, c_2), ...) where I is interference term from Wickens MRT
operators.context: Delta(c_ctx, c_target) = (mu_target * (1 + alpha * H(c_ctx)), ...) where H is entropy of context

cognitive_cost_algebra.soundness: The algebra provides an upper bound on the true mutual information of the full discrete-event simulation: C_algebra(tau) >= I(S;A|tau) for all trajectories tau

bisimulation_theory.description: State-space reduction preserving behavioral equivalence under bounded rationality
bisimulation_theory.cognitive_distance: d_cog(s, s') = sup_{pi in Pi_beta} |V^pi(s) - V^pi(s')| where Pi_beta is the set of beta-bounded-rational policies
bisimulation_theory.partition_refinement: Iterative refinement of partition blocks until d_cog < epsilon within each block
bisimulation_theory.preservation: For any pair of states in the same block: |C(tau_s) - C(tau_{s'})| < epsilon under all beta-bounded-rational policies
bisimulation_theory.complexity_reduction: Empirically reduces 10^4-10^6 raw states to <=10^4 abstract states for production UIs

bottleneck_taxonomy.description: Classification of usability regressions by information-theoretic channel signatures

types.name: perceptual_overload
types.description: Visual search or identification demand exceeds perceptual capacity

types.name: choice_paralysis
types.description: Number of alternatives exceeds choice capacity (Hick-Hyman)

types.name: motor_difficulty
types.description: Target acquisition difficulty exceeds motor capacity (Fitts')

types.name: cross_channel_interference
types.signature: I(S;A|total) > sum C_i despite I(S;A|i) < C_i for all i
types.description: Individual channels within capacity but interference causes overflow

definitions.name: Task-State MDP
definitions.formal_statement: $M = (S, A, P, R, \gamma, s_0, S_G)$ where $S$ is the state space (UI configurations), $A$ is the action space (user interactions), $P: S \times A \to \Delta(S)$ is the transition kernel, $R: S \times A \to \mathbb{R}$ is the reward function (negative cognitive cost), $\gamma \in [0,1)$ is the discount factor, $s_0$ is the initial state, and $S_G \subseteq S$ is the goal set.
definitions.informal_description: An MDP encoding UI navigation where states represent UI configurations (element focus, scroll position, modal state) and actions represent user interactions (click, type, scroll, tab).

definitions.name: Bounded-Rational Policy
definitions.formal_statement: $\pi_\beta(a|s) = \frac{p_0(a|s) \exp(\beta Q^*(s,a))}{Z_\beta(s)}$ where $Z_\beta(s) = \sum_{a'} p_0(a'|s) \exp(\beta Q^*(s,a'))$ is the partition function and $Q^*$ satisfies $Q^*(s,a) = R(s,a) + \gamma \sum_{s'} P(s'|s,a) V^*(s')$ with $V^*(s) = \frac{1}{\beta} \log Z_\beta(s)$.
definitions.informal_description: The optimal policy under information-processing constraints: a softmax over Q-values scaled by the rationality parameter beta, with prior policy p_0 as the reference.

definitions.name: Cognitive Cost Tuple
definitions.formal_statement: $c = (\mu, \sigma^2, \kappa, \mathbf{d})$ where $\mu \in \mathbb{R}_{\geq 0}$ is mean cost, $\sigma^2 \in \mathbb{R}_{\geq 0}$ is variance, $\kappa \in \mathbb{R}$ is excess kurtosis, and $\mathbf{d} \in \mathbb{R}^K_{\geq 0}$ is the resource demand vector across $K$ cognitive channels.
definitions.informal_description: A structured representation of cognitive effort capturing not just expected time but also variability, tail risk, and multi-resource demands.

definitions.name: Sequential Composition
definitions.formal_statement: $c_1 \oplus c_2 = (\mu_1 + \mu_2 + \gamma_{12}, \sigma_1^2 + \sigma_2^2 + 2\rho_{12}\sigma_1\sigma_2, \kappa_{\oplus}, \max(\mathbf{d}_1, \mathbf{d}_2))$ where $\gamma_{12}$ is the context-dependent coupling term and $\rho_{12}$ is the correlation between sequential stages.
definitions.informal_description: Composing two sequential cognitive operations, accounting for the fact that the second operation's cost depends on the cognitive state after the first.

definitions.name: Parallel Interference
definitions.formal_statement: $c_1 \otimes c_2 = (\max(\mu_1, \mu_2) + I(c_1, c_2), \sigma_1^2 + \sigma_2^2 + \sigma_I^2, \kappa_{\otimes}, \mathbf{d}_1 + \mathbf{d}_2)$ where $I(c_1, c_2) = \sum_{k=1}^{K} w_k \cdot \mathbb{1}[d_{1,k} > 0 \wedge d_{2,k} > 0] \cdot f(d_{1,k}, d_{2,k})$ is the Wickens interference term.
definitions.informal_description: Composing two concurrent cognitive operations, where time is the maximum of individual times plus an interference penalty when operations compete for the same resources.

definitions.name: Context Modulation
definitions.formal_statement: $\Delta(c_{\text{ctx}}, c_{\text{target}}) = (\mu_{\text{target}} \cdot (1 + \alpha \cdot H(c_{\text{ctx}})), \sigma_{\text{target}}^2 \cdot (1 + \alpha^2 \cdot H(c_{\text{ctx}})^2), \kappa_{\Delta}, \mathbf{d}_{\text{target}})$ where $H(c_{\text{ctx}})$ is the entropy of the context cost distribution and $\alpha > 0$ is the modulation strength.
definitions.informal_description: The effect of prior cognitive context on a subsequent operation: higher context entropy (uncertainty) amplifies the cost of the target operation.

definitions.name: Cognitive Distance
definitions.formal_statement: $d_{\text{cog}}(s, s') = \sup_{\pi \in \Pi_\beta} |V^\pi(s) - V^\pi(s')| = \sup_{\pi \in \Pi_\beta} |\mathbb{E}_\pi[\sum_{t=0}^{\infty} \gamma^t R(s_t, a_t) | s_0 = s] - \mathbb{E}_\pi[\sum_{t=0}^{\infty} \gamma^t R(s_t, a_t) | s_0 = s']|$
definitions.informal_description: The maximum difference in expected cumulative reward between two states, taken over all bounded-rational policies. States that are behaviorally indistinguishable to a capacity-limited agent have small cognitive distance.

definitions.name: Bisimulation Partition
definitions.formal_statement: $\mathcal{P}_\epsilon = \{B_1, \ldots, B_m\}$ is an $\epsilon$-bisimulation partition of $S$ if for all $i$ and all $s, s' \in B_i$: (1) $d_{\text{cog}}(s, s') < \epsilon$, (2) for all $a \in A$ and all $j$: $|P(B_j | s, a) - P(B_j | s', a)| < \epsilon$, and (3) $|R(s, a) - R(s', a)| < \epsilon$ for all $a$.
definitions.informal_description: A partition of the state space into blocks where states within each block are behaviorally equivalent up to tolerance epsilon under bounded-rational policies.

definitions.name: Quotient MDP
definitions.formal_statement: $\tilde{M} = (\tilde{S}, A, \tilde{P}, \tilde{R}, \gamma)$ where $\tilde{S} = \{B_1, \ldots, B_m\}$, $\tilde{P}(B_j | B_i, a) = \frac{1}{|B_i|} \sum_{s \in B_i} P(B_j | s, a)$, and $\tilde{R}(B_i, a) = \frac{1}{|B_i|} \sum_{s \in B_i} R(s, a)$.
definitions.informal_description: The abstract MDP obtained by collapsing each bisimulation block to a single state, with averaged transition probabilities and rewards.

definitions.name: Channel Capacity
definitions.formal_statement: $C_i = \max_{p(x)} I(X; Y_i) = \max_{p(x)} \sum_{x,y} p(x) p(y|x) \log \frac{p(y|x)}{p(y)}$ where the maximum is over input distributions to cognitive channel $i$.
definitions.informal_description: The maximum rate at which information can be reliably transmitted through a cognitive subsystem (e.g., motor, perceptual, choice channel).

definitions.name: Fitts' Channel Model
definitions.formal_statement: $C_{\text{motor}} = \frac{ID}{MT} = \frac{\log_2(1 + D/W)}{a + b \cdot \log_2(1 + D/W)}$ where $ID$ is the index of difficulty, $D$ is movement distance, $W$ is target width, and $a, b$ are empirical parameters.
definitions.informal_description: Fitts' law recast as a channel capacity: the throughput in bits per second of the human motor system for aimed movements.

definitions.name: Hick-Hyman Channel Model
definitions.formal_statement: $C_{\text{choice}} = \frac{H(X)}{RT} = \frac{-\sum_{i=1}^{n} p_i \log_2 p_i}{a + b \cdot H(X)}$ where $H(X)$ is the stimulus entropy, $n$ is the number of alternatives, $p_i$ are stimulus probabilities.
definitions.informal_description: The Hick-Hyman law recast as a choice channel capacity: bits of choice information processed per second.

definitions.name: Regression Verdict Function
definitions.formal_statement: $V(M_A, M_B, \tau, \alpha) = \begin{cases} \text{REGRESSION} & \text{if } p_{\text{paired}} < \alpha \text{ and } \hat{\Delta} > 0 \\ \text{IMPROVEMENT} & \text{if } p_{\text{paired}} < \alpha \text{ and } \hat{\Delta} < 0 \\ \text{PASS} & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$ where $\hat{\Delta} = \hat{C}(M_B, \tau) - \hat{C}(M_A, \tau)$.
definitions.informal_description: The regression decision function: compare costs of two UI versions on task tau using paired statistical tests at significance level alpha.

definitions.name: Bottleneck Signature
definitions.formal_statement: $\mathcal{B}(\Delta) = \arg\max_{i \in \{1,\ldots,K\}} \frac{\Delta I_i}{C_i}$ where $\Delta I_i = I_i(M_B) - I_i(M_A)$ is the change in mutual information demand on channel $i$.
definitions.informal_description: The bottleneck type is identified by the channel whose capacity utilization increased the most relative to its capacity.

definitions.name: Repair Constraint System
definitions.formal_statement: $\Phi(T') = \Phi_{\text{struct}}(T') \wedge \Phi_{\text{valid}}(T') \wedge \Phi_{\text{cost}}(T') \wedge \Phi_{\text{task}}(T')$ subject to minimize $d_{\text{edit}}(T, T')$ where $T$ is the original tree, $T'$ is the repaired tree, and $\Phi$ encodes structural, validity, cost, and task-completability constraints.
definitions.informal_description: The SMT constraint system for repair synthesis: find a minimally modified accessibility tree that satisfies all structural and cost constraints.

definitions.name: Information-Theoretic Cost
definitions.formal_statement: $C_{\text{IT}}(s, a, s') = \sum_{i=1}^{K} \frac{1}{\beta_i} \log \frac{\pi_i(a_i | s)}{p_{0,i}(a_i | s)}$ where the sum is over cognitive subsystems and $a_i$ is the component of action $a$ processed by subsystem $i$.
definitions.informal_description: The information-theoretic cost of a transition: the total KL divergence from prior across all cognitive channels, weighted by inverse capacities.

definitions.name: Affine Arithmetic Form
definitions.formal_statement: $\hat{x} = x_0 + \sum_{i=1}^{n} x_i \varepsilon_i$ where $x_0 \in \mathbb{R}$ is the central value, $x_i \in \mathbb{R}$ are noise coefficients, and $\varepsilon_i \in [-1, 1]$ are noise symbols.
definitions.informal_description: Affine arithmetic representation tracking correlations between variables for tighter interval bounds than standard interval arithmetic.

definitions.name: Wickens Resource Demand Vector
definitions.formal_statement: $\mathbf{d} = (d_{\text{perc}}, d_{\text{cog}}, d_{\text{resp}}, d_{\text{vis}}, d_{\text{aud}}, d_{\text{spat}}, d_{\text{verb}}, d_{\text{focal}}, d_{\text{ambient}})$ where each component represents demand on a dimension of the Wickens 4-D multiple resource model.
definitions.informal_description: A vector quantifying the resource demands of a cognitive task across Wickens' four dimensions: processing stage, modality, code, and visual channel.

definitions.name: Bounded-Rational Scheduling
definitions.formal_statement: Given tasks $\{T_i\}$ with deadlines $\{d_i\}$, a bounded-rational scheduler selects ordering $\sigma$ minimizing modified free energy $F_{\text{sched}}(\sigma) = \sum_i c(T_{\sigma(i)}) + \lambda \sum_i u(T_{\sigma(i)}, d_{\sigma(i)})$ where $u(T,d) = \exp(\alpha (t_{\text{completion}}(T) - d))$ models deadline pressure and $\lambda$ trades off cognitive cost against urgency.
definitions.informal_description: Task scheduling under cognitive constraints where deadline pressure is modeled as a modified free-energy term, combining cognitive execution cost with an exponential urgency function.

definitions.name: Channel Capacity Mapping (Wickens MRT)
definitions.formal_statement: A channel capacity mapping $\Phi: \mathcal{R} \to \mathbb{R}_+$ assigns to each Wickens MRT resource $r \in \mathcal{R} = \{r_{\text{vis}}, r_{\text{aud}}, r_{\text{cog}}, r_{\text{motor}}\}$ a channel capacity $C_r = \max_{p(x)} I(X; Y_r)$. Dual-task interference is bounded by $C_{\text{total}}(r) \leq C_r / (1 + \eta_{r,r'} d_{r'})$ where $\eta_{r,r'}$ is the Wickens interference coefficient.
definitions.informal_description: Wickens' Multiple Resource Theory resources modeled as information-theoretic channels, each with measurable capacity subject to cross-resource interference.

definitions.name: Differential Privacy for Usability Data
definitions.formal_statement: A randomized mechanism $\mathcal{M}: \mathcal{D} \to \mathcal{O}$ satisfies $(\varepsilon, \delta)$-differential privacy for usability data if for all neighboring datasets $D, D'$ (differing in one user session) and all measurable $O \subseteq \mathcal{O}$: $\Pr[\mathcal{M}(D) \in O] \leq e^{\varepsilon} \Pr[\mathcal{M}(D') \in O] + \delta$.
definitions.informal_description: Privacy-preserving aggregation of usability metrics ensuring that the inclusion or exclusion of any single user's data has bounded influence on published results.

definitions.name: GOMS-to-Bounded-Rationality Bridge
definitions.formal_statement: For a GOMS model $G = (Goals, Operators, Methods, Selection)$ with KLM prediction time $T_{\text{KLM}}(G)$, the bridge mapping $\Psi: G \mapsto \pi^*_\beta$ yields a bounded-rational policy whose expected cost satisfies $\mathbb{E}_{\pi^*_\beta}[C(\tau)] = T_{\text{KLM}}(G) \cdot (1 + O(1/\beta))$, relating classical HCI task analysis to free-energy-optimal behavior.
definitions.informal_description: A formal mapping from GOMS/KLM task models to bounded-rational policies, showing that KLM predicted times approximate free-energy-optimal costs in the high-rationality limit.

definitions.name: Rate-Distortion Bisimulation
definitions.formal_statement: Given MDP $M$ and distortion measure $d: S \times \hat{S} \to \mathbb{R}_+$, the rate-distortion bisimulation at rate $R$ is the partition $\mathcal{B}_R = \arg\min_{|\hat{S}| \leq 2^R} \mathbb{E}[d(s, \hat{s})]$ subject to $I(S; \hat{S}) \leq R$, yielding the coarsest state aggregation within distortion budget.
definitions.informal_description: State-space bisimulation interpreted as rate-distortion coding: the quotient MDP is the optimal lossy compression of the state space at a given information rate.

definitions.name: Cognitive Bisimulation Metric
definitions.formal_statement: $d_{\text{cog}}: S \times S \to \mathbb{R}_+$ is defined as $d_{\text{cog}}(s, s') = \max_a |c(s,a) - c(s',a)| + \gamma \cdot W_1(P(\cdot|s,a), P(\cdot|s',a))$ where $W_1$ is the Wasserstein-1 distance and $c$ is the cognitive cost function. $d_{\text{cog}}$ is the greatest fixpoint of this recursive equation.
definitions.informal_description: A pseudometric on states measuring behavioral distance in terms of cognitive cost differences and transition probability discrepancy, generalizing exact bisimulation to a quantitative notion.

definitions.name: Probabilistic Bisimulation with Capacity Weighting
definitions.formal_statement: Two states $s_1, s_2$ are $(\varepsilon, \mathbf{C})$-bisimilar if $|c_i(s_1,a) - c_i(s_2,a)| \leq \varepsilon / C_i$ for all actions $a$ and channels $i$, where $C_i$ is the channel capacity. Equivalently, $d_{\text{cog}}^{\mathbf{C}}(s_1, s_2) = \max_i C_i |c_i(s_1,a) - c_i(s_2,a)|$.
definitions.informal_description: A capacity-weighted bisimulation where cost differences on higher-capacity channels are penalized more, reflecting that higher-bandwidth cognitive channels are more sensitive to perturbation.

definitions.name: ACT-R Integration Model
definitions.formal_statement: An ACT-R integration maps declarative memory activations $A_i = B_i + \sum_j W_j S_{ji} + \varepsilon$ and procedural conflict resolution $P(\text{fire}_i) \propto e^{U_i / t}$ into bounded-rational policy parameters: $\beta = 1/t$ (temperature as rationality) and chunk retrieval cost $c_{\text{ret}}(s) = F e^{-A_i}$ (latency-cost correspondence).
definitions.informal_description: Integration of ACT-R cognitive architecture parameters into the bounded-rationality framework, mapping ACT-R noise temperature to rationality parameter and retrieval latency to cognitive cost.

definitions.name: Operadic Cost Composition
definitions.formal_statement: An operad $\mathcal{O}_C$ of cognitive costs has operations $\theta_n: C^n \to C$ for each $n$-ary task composition pattern. Sequential composition is $\theta_{\text{seq}}(c_1, c_2) = c_1 \oplus c_2$, parallel is $\theta_{\text{par}}(c_1, c_2) = c_1 \otimes c_2$, and hierarchical nesting satisfies the associativity axiom $\theta_m(\theta_{n_1}(\ldots), \ldots) = \theta_{n_1 + \cdots + n_m}(\ldots)$.
definitions.informal_description: A category-theoretic operadic structure on cognitive cost composition, ensuring that hierarchical nesting of task costs is associative and compositional across sequential, parallel, and nested patterns.

definitions.name: Category-Theoretic Cost Algebra
definitions.formal_statement: The cost algebra forms a symmetric monoidal category $(\mathbf{Cost}, \oplus, \mathbf{0})$ where objects are cost tuples, morphisms are cost-preserving transformations, $\oplus$ is sequential composition (monoidal product), and the braiding $\sigma: c_1 \oplus c_2 \cong c_2 \oplus c_1$ holds up to commutativity of independent costs. Parallel composition is a second monoidal structure $\otimes$ making $\mathbf{Cost}$ a rig category.
definitions.informal_description: The cognitive cost algebra organized as a rig category with two monoidal structures (sequential and parallel), providing a rigorous compositional semantics for multi-level task cost aggregation.

theorems.name: Cognitive Cost Algebra Soundness
theorems.formal_statement: For any trajectory $\tau = (s_0, a_0, s_1, a_1, \ldots, s_T)$ in MDP $M$, the algebraic cost $C_{\text{alg}}(\tau) = \bigoplus_{t=0}^{T-1} c(s_t, a_t)$ satisfies $C_{\text{alg}}(\tau).\mu \geq I(S_{0:T}; A_{0:T-1})$, i.e., the mean algebraic cost upper-bounds the true mutual information of the trajectory.
theorems.proof_sketch: By the data processing inequality and the chain rule for mutual information. Each term c(s_t, a_t).mu >= I(S_t; A_t | S_{0:t-1}, A_{0:t-1}) by construction from the channel capacity models. The coupling terms gamma_{t,t+1} account for conditional mutual information between consecutive steps. Summing and applying the chain rule yields the total bound.

theorems.name: Bisimulation Preservation
theorems.formal_statement: Let $\mathcal{P}_\epsilon$ be an $\epsilon$-bisimulation partition and $\tilde{M}$ the quotient MDP. For any $\beta$-bounded-rational policy $\pi_\beta$ and task $\tau$: $|C_{\tilde{M}}(\tau) - C_M(\tau)| \leq \frac{2\epsilon}{(1-\gamma)^2}$.
theorems.proof_sketch: By the simulation lemma (Kearns & Singh 2002) adapted to bounded rationality. The key step is showing that the softmax policy in the quotient MDP is epsilon-close to the aggregated policy in the original MDP, which follows from the Lipschitz continuity of the softmax operator and the bounded KL divergence within each block.

theorems.name: Soft Value Iteration Convergence
theorems.formal_statement: The soft Bellman operator $\mathcal{T}_\beta V(s) = \frac{1}{\beta} \log \sum_a p_0(a|s) \exp(\beta [R(s,a) + \gamma \sum_{s'} P(s'|s,a) V(s')])$ is a $\gamma$-contraction in the sup-norm. Hence soft value iteration converges geometrically: $\|V_n - V^*\|_\infty \leq \gamma^n \|V_0 - V^*\|_\infty$.
theorems.proof_sketch: The log-sum-exp function is non-expansive: |logsumexp(x) - logsumexp(y)| <= ||x - y||_inf. Applying this to the soft Bellman operator, the gamma factor from discounting yields the contraction. Banach fixed-point theorem gives geometric convergence.

theorems.name: Paired Comparison Theorem
theorems.formal_statement: Under shared bisimulation partition $\mathcal{P}$, the variance of the cost difference $\Delta C = C(M_B, \tau) - C(M_A, \tau)$ satisfies $\text{Var}(\Delta C) \leq \text{Var}(C_A) + \text{Var}(C_B) - 2\rho \sqrt{\text{Var}(C_A)\text{Var}(C_B)}$ where $\rho \geq 1 - O(\epsilon)$ is the correlation induced by shared bisimulation.
theorems.proof_sketch: Shared bisimulation means both policies operate on the same abstract MDP structure. Cost samples from M_A and M_B using the same trajectory seeds share the same state transitions up to epsilon perturbation. By the correlation inequality for coupled random variables and the bound on within-block variation.

theorems.name: FDR Control
theorems.formal_statement: When testing $m$ task specifications simultaneously using the Benjamini-Hochberg procedure at level $\alpha$, the false discovery rate satisfies $\text{FDR} = \mathbb{E}[V/\max(R,1)] \leq \alpha \cdot m_0 / m \leq \alpha$ where $V$ is the number of false rejections, $R$ is the total rejections, and $m_0$ is the number of true nulls.
theorems.proof_sketch: Under the assumption that p-values corresponding to true null hypotheses are independent of each other and of the non-null p-values (PRDS condition), the BH procedure controls FDR at level alpha * m_0/m by the Benjamini-Hochberg (1995) theorem. The cost differences under the null are independent across tasks by the MDP structure.

theorems.name: Repair Minimality
theorems.formal_statement: If the MaxSMT solver returns a repair $T'$ satisfying all hard constraints $\Phi(T')$, then $d_{\text{edit}}(T, T')$ is minimal among all feasible repairs: $d_{\text{edit}}(T, T') = \min_{T'' : \Phi(T'') \text{ SAT}} d_{\text{edit}}(T, T'')$.
theorems.proof_sketch: By construction, the MaxSMT formulation encodes each potential edit as a soft constraint with unit weight. The MaxSMT solver maximizes the number of satisfied soft constraints, which is equivalent to minimizing the number of edits. Optimality of the MaxSMT solution follows from the completeness of the Z3 Optimize engine.

theorems.name: Complexity Bound
theorems.formal_statement: The full pipeline (parsing, MDP construction, bisimulation, cost computation, policy computation, comparison) runs in time $O(|T|^2 \cdot |A| \cdot K \cdot N_{\text{iter}} + |\tilde{S}|^2 \cdot |A| \cdot N_{\text{VI}})$ where $|T|$ is the accessibility tree size, $K$ is the number of cognitive channels, $N_{\text{iter}}$ is bisimulation iterations, $|\tilde{S}|$ is the quotient state count, and $N_{\text{VI}}$ is value iteration steps.
theorems.proof_sketch: Parsing is O(|T|). MDP construction is O(|T|^2 |A|) in the worst case. Bisimulation partition refinement is O(|S|^2 K N_iter) where N_iter is typically O(log |S|). Value iteration on the quotient is O(|S_tilde|^2 |A| N_VI) where N_VI = O(log(1/epsilon) / log(1/gamma)). MC sampling is O(N_samples * max_steps). Total is dominated by the quadratic terms.

theorems.name: Blahut-Arimoto Convergence
theorems.formal_statement: The Blahut-Arimoto algorithm for computing channel capacity $C = \max_{p(x)} I(X;Y)$ converges at rate $|C_n - C| = O(1/n)$ where $n$ is the iteration count.
theorems.proof_sketch: Blahut-Arimoto is an instance of alternating maximization on a concave function. By the general theory of EM-type algorithms, the sequence of lower bounds C_n is monotonically non-decreasing and converges to the global maximum. The O(1/n) rate follows from the strong concavity of mutual information in the input distribution.

theorems.name: Multi-Resource Interference Bound
theorems.formal_statement: For tasks with demand vectors $\mathbf{d}_1, \ldots, \mathbf{d}_n$, the total interference $I_{\text{total}} = \sum_{i<j} I(\mathbf{d}_i, \mathbf{d}_j)$ satisfies $I_{\text{total}} \leq \binom{n}{2} \cdot \max_{i<j} I(\mathbf{d}_i, \mathbf{d}_j)$ and is subadditive: $I(\mathbf{d}_1 + \mathbf{d}_2, \mathbf{d}_3) \leq I(\mathbf{d}_1, \mathbf{d}_3) + I(\mathbf{d}_2, \mathbf{d}_3)$.
theorems.proof_sketch: The first bound is trivial. Subadditivity follows from the linearity of the Wickens conflict function in the demand magnitudes and the fact that the conflict weights w_k are non-negative constants. The detailed proof decomposes the interference by resource dimension and uses the triangle inequality.

theorems.name: Channel Capacity Composition
theorems.formal_statement: For $K$ cognitive channels in series, the effective capacity satisfies $C_{\text{serial}} = \min_{i=1}^{K} C_i$. For channels in parallel with independent noise, $C_{\text{parallel}} = \sum_{i=1}^{K} C_i$.
theorems.proof_sketch: Serial composition: the bottleneck channel limits throughput by the data processing inequality. Parallel composition: independent channels can transmit information simultaneously, and the total capacity is the sum by the independence bound on mutual information. The proof uses the chain rule for mutual information and the fact that capacity-achieving input distributions can be computed independently for each channel.

theorems.name: Regression Detection Power
theorems.formal_statement: For a regression of effect size $\delta$ (Cohen's d), the paired t-test with $n$ trajectory samples has power $\text{Power} = \Phi\left(\frac{\delta\sqrt{n} - z_{\alpha/2}}{1}\right)$ where $\Phi$ is the standard normal CDF and $z_{\alpha/2}$ is the critical value.
theorems.proof_sketch: Under the alternative hypothesis H_1: Delta = delta * sigma, the test statistic t = Delta_hat / (s / sqrt(n)) follows a non-central t-distribution with non-centrality parameter lambda = delta * sqrt(n). For large n, this approximates a normal distribution shifted by lambda.

theorems.name: Affine Arithmetic Tightness
theorems.formal_statement: For any expression $f(x_1, \ldots, x_k)$ that is a composition of affine operations, the affine arithmetic evaluation $\hat{f}$ produces an interval $[\hat{f}]$ satisfying $[\hat{f}] \subseteq [f]_{\text{interval}}$ where $[f]_{\text{interval}}$ is the standard interval arithmetic evaluation, with equality when $f$ is affine.
theorems.proof_sketch: Affine arithmetic tracks linear correlations between variables through noise symbols epsilon_i. For affine operations, this tracking is exact. For non-affine operations, the linearization introduces conservative overapproximation, but the correlation tracking still yields tighter bounds than naive interval arithmetic which treats all variables as independent.

theorems.name: Wickens MRT Channel Capacity Bound
theorems.formal_statement: For a dual-task scenario with Wickens resource demands $\mathbf{d}_1, \mathbf{d}_2$, the total cognitive throughput is bounded by $\text{Throughput}(\mathbf{d}_1 + \mathbf{d}_2) \leq \sum_{r \in \mathcal{R}} C_r \cdot \min\!\bigl(1,\; (d_{1,r} + d_{2,r})^{-1}\bigr)$ where $C_r$ are channel capacities. Equality holds when resource demands are non-overlapping.
theorems.proof_sketch: Each Wickens resource is modeled as an independent channel with capacity C_r. By the data-processing inequality, the total information processed cannot exceed the sum of individual channel capacities. When demands overlap, the effective capacity is reduced by the interference factor derived from the Wickens conflict matrix. The water-filling allocation theorem gives the achievable bound.

theorems.name: DP Usability Aggregation Utility
theorems.formal_statement: For an $(\varepsilon, \delta)$-DP mechanism aggregating $n$ user sessions with true regression indicator $R \in \{0,1\}$, the private regression test achieves power $1 - \beta_{\text{priv}} \geq 1 - \beta_0 - O\!\bigl(\sqrt{\log(1/\delta)} / (\varepsilon \sqrt{n})\bigr)$ where $\beta_0$ is the non-private type-II error. Regression detection is preserved with bounded error for $n = \Omega(\log(1/\delta) / \varepsilon^2)$.
theorems.proof_sketch: The private mechanism adds Gaussian noise with scale proportional to sensitivity/epsilon. By the central limit theorem, the aggregate test statistic concentrates around its true mean. The additional variance from privacy noise inflates the type-II error by a term that decreases as O(1/(epsilon sqrt(n))), establishing the sample-size requirement for negligible utility loss.

theorems.name: GOMS-to-Bounded-Rationality Correspondence
theorems.formal_statement: For GOMS model $G$ with KLM time prediction $T_{\text{KLM}}(G)$ and corresponding bounded-rational policy $\pi^*_\beta$ on MDP $M_G$, the expected cost satisfies $\bigl|\mathbb{E}_{\pi^*_\beta}[C(\tau)] - T_{\text{KLM}}(G)\bigr| \leq \frac{H(\pi^*_\beta)}{\beta}$ where $H(\pi^*_\beta)$ is the policy entropy. As $\beta \to \infty$, the bounded-rational cost converges to the KLM prediction.
theorems.proof_sketch: The free-energy functional is F = E[C] - H/beta. At optimality, E[C] = F* + H*/beta. The KLM prediction corresponds to the deterministic optimal cost (beta -> infinity limit). The entropy term H*/beta quantifies the deviation due to bounded rationality. The bound follows from the non-negativity of entropy and the free-energy optimality condition.

theorems.name: Rate-Distortion Optimal Bisimulation
theorems.formal_statement: The bisimulation quotient $\hat{M}$ of MDP $M$ at partition $\mathcal{B}_R$ achieves the rate-distortion function: $D(R) = \min_{I(S;\hat{S}) \leq R} \mathbb{E}[d_{\text{cog}}(s, \hat{s})]$. Furthermore, for any $\varepsilon$-bisimulation partition $\mathcal{B}_\varepsilon$, $|\hat{S}_\varepsilon| \geq 2^{R(D^{-1}(\varepsilon))}$, establishing a lower bound on quotient size.
theorems.proof_sketch: The bisimulation partition groups states with similar cognitive costs and transitions. This is equivalent to vector quantization of the state space under d_cog. By Shannon's rate-distortion theorem, the minimum number of codewords (abstract states) to achieve distortion epsilon is 2^{R(epsilon)}. The quotient MDP construction achieves this bound because bisimulation equivalence is the finest partition satisfying the distortion constraint.

theorems.name: Cognitive Bisimulation Metric Validity
theorems.formal_statement: $d_{\text{cog}}$ is a valid pseudometric on $S$: (i) $d_{\text{cog}}(s,s) = 0$, (ii) $d_{\text{cog}}(s,s') = d_{\text{cog}}(s',s)$, (iii) $d_{\text{cog}}(s,s'') \leq d_{\text{cog}}(s,s') + d_{\text{cog}}(s',s'')$. Moreover, $d_{\text{cog}}(s,s') = 0$ iff $s$ and $s'$ are cognitively bisimilar.
theorems.proof_sketch: Properties (i) and (ii) follow directly from the definition. The triangle inequality (iii) follows from the triangle inequality of the Wasserstein metric and the max over actions. The characterization of the zero set follows from Banach's fixpoint theorem applied to the contraction mapping defining d_cog (with contraction factor gamma < 1).

theorems.name: Operadic Composition Soundness
theorems.formal_statement: For any operadic composition $\theta \in \mathcal{O}_C$ of costs $c_1, \ldots, c_n$ with associated task trajectories $\tau_1, \ldots, \tau_n$, the composed cost satisfies $\theta(c_1, \ldots, c_n).\mu \geq I(S_{1:n}; A_{1:n})$ (information-theoretic lower bound) and $\theta(c_1, \ldots, c_n).\mu \leq \sum_i c_i.\mu + \sum_{i < j} \eta_{ij} \sqrt{c_i.\sigma^2 c_j.\sigma^2}$ (interference upper bound).
theorems.proof_sketch: The lower bound extends T1 (cost algebra soundness) to operadic compositions by induction on the operadic tree structure: leaves are individual costs satisfying T1, and each internal node preserves the information-theoretic bound by the data-processing inequality. The upper bound follows from Cauchy-Schwarz applied to cross-task interference terms, with eta_ij being the Wickens interference coefficients.

lemmas.name: Softmax Lipschitz Continuity
lemmas.formal_statement: The softmax function $\sigma_\beta(x)_i = \exp(\beta x_i) / \sum_j \exp(\beta x_j)$ is Lipschitz continuous with constant $\beta$: $\|\sigma_\beta(x) - \sigma_\beta(y)\|_1 \leq \beta \|x - y\|_\infty$.
lemmas.proof_sketch: By the mean value theorem and the bound on the Jacobian of the softmax: ||J_sigma||_{1,inf} <= beta.

lemmas.name: KL Divergence Monotonicity under Marginalization
lemmas.formal_statement: $D_{\text{KL}}(p(x) \| q(x)) \leq D_{\text{KL}}(p(x,y) \| q(x,y))$ (information monotonicity / data processing inequality for KL).
lemmas.proof_sketch: By the chain rule for KL: D_KL(p(x,y)||q(x,y)) = D_KL(p(x)||q(x)) + E_{p(x)}[D_KL(p(y|x)||q(y|x))]. The second term is non-negative.

lemmas.name: Value Function Bound
lemmas.formal_statement: For any policy $\pi$ and bounded rewards $|R| \leq R_{\max}$: $|V^\pi(s)| \leq \frac{R_{\max}}{1 - \gamma}$ for all states $s$.
lemmas.proof_sketch: By geometric series: |V^pi(s)| <= sum_{t=0}^inf gamma^t R_max = R_max / (1-gamma).

lemmas.name: Partition Refinement Termination
lemmas.formal_statement: The bisimulation partition refinement algorithm terminates in at most $|S|$ iterations, producing the coarsest $\epsilon$-bisimulation partition.
lemmas.proof_sketch: Each iteration either refines (splits) at least one block or the algorithm terminates. Since there are at most |S| blocks, at most |S| splits can occur. The coarsest property follows from the fixpoint characterization of bisimulation.

lemmas.name: Cost Tuple Associativity
lemmas.formal_statement: The sequential composition operator $\oplus$ is associative up to $O(\epsilon^2)$: $\|(c_1 \oplus c_2) \oplus c_3 - c_1 \oplus (c_2 \oplus c_3)\| \leq \epsilon^2$ where $\epsilon$ bounds the coupling terms.
lemmas.proof_sketch: The mean component is exactly associative: (mu_1 + mu_2) + mu_3 = mu_1 + (mu_2 + mu_3). The coupling terms gamma introduce O(epsilon) perturbations, and the composition of two such perturbations is O(epsilon^2).

lemmas.name: Importance Sampling Variance
lemmas.formal_statement: For importance sampling with proposal $q$ and target $p$, the variance of the importance-weighted estimator satisfies $\text{Var}_q[\frac{p(x)}{q(x)} f(x)] = \mathbb{E}_p[f(x)^2 \frac{p(x)}{q(x)}] - (\mathbb{E}_p[f(x)])^2$.
lemmas.proof_sketch: Direct computation: Var_q[w*f] = E_q[(w*f)^2] - (E_q[w*f])^2 where w = p/q. Substituting and using the change of measure E_q[g(x)p(x)/q(x)] = E_p[g(x)].

lemmas.name: Gelman-Rubin Convergence Criterion
lemmas.formal_statement: If the potential scale reduction factor $\hat{R} = \sqrt{(n-1)/n + B/(nW)} < 1.1$ for all monitored quantities, where $B$ is the between-chain variance and $W$ is the within-chain variance, then the chains have approximately converged to the stationary distribution.
lemmas.proof_sketch: R-hat measures the ratio of the total variance estimate to the within-chain variance. When chains have converged, B/W approaches 0 and R-hat approaches 1. The threshold 1.1 is a conventional criterion from Gelman & Rubin (1992).

algorithms.name: Soft Value Iteration
algorithms.description: Compute bounded-rational value function via iterative application of the soft Bellman operator
algorithms.complexity: O(|S|^2 |A| N_iter) where N_iter = O(log(1/epsilon) / log(1/gamma))
inputs: MDP M = (S, A, P, R, gamma)
pseudocode_steps: Initialize V_0(s) = 0 for all s
Repeat until ||V_{n+1} - V_n||_inf < epsilon:
For each state s:
For each action a: Q(s,a) = R(s,a) + gamma * sum_{s'} P(s'|s,a) V_n(s')
V_{n+1}(s) = (1/beta) * log(sum_a p_0(a|s) * exp(beta * Q(s,a)))
Extract policy: pi(a|s) = p_0(a|s) * exp(beta * Q(s,a)) / Z(s)

algorithms.name: Blahut-Arimoto Channel Capacity
algorithms.description: Compute channel capacity C = max_{p(x)} I(X;Y) via alternating maximization
algorithms.complexity: O(|X| |Y| N_iter) where N_iter = O(1/epsilon) for epsilon-accuracy
pseudocode_steps: Initialize p_0(x) = 1/|X| (uniform)
Repeat until |C_n - C_{n-1}| < epsilon:
Compute: q(x|y) = p_n(x) P(y|x) / sum_{x'} p_n(x') P(y|x')
Compute: c(x) = exp(sum_y P(y|x) log q(x|y))
Update: p_{n+1}(x) = c(x) / sum_{x'} c(x')
Compute: C_n = log(sum_x c(x))

algorithms.name: Bisimulation Partition Refinement
algorithms.description: Compute the coarsest epsilon-bisimulation partition via iterative block splitting
algorithms.complexity: O(|S|^2 |A| K) where K is the number of refinement iterations (at most |S|)
pseudocode_steps: Initialize P_0 = {S} (single block)
Repeat until P_{n+1} = P_n:
For each block B in P_n:
For each pair (s, s') in B:
Compute d_cog(s, s') using current V_beta
If d_cog(s, s') > epsilon: mark for splitting
Split B into sub-blocks of epsilon-close states
Recompute V_beta on the refined partition
Construct quotient MDP from final partition

algorithms.name: Forward-Backward Cost Algebra Evaluation
algorithms.description: Evaluate compositional cognitive cost over a trajectory using forward and backward passes
algorithms.complexity: O(T * K) where T is trajectory length and K is the number of cognitive channels
pseudocode_steps: Forward pass: compute per-step costs c(s_t, a_t) for t = 0, ..., T-1
For each step: decompose into channel costs (motor, choice, visual, memory)
Apply Fitts' law, Hick-Hyman, visual search, WM models
Backward pass: compute context modulation Delta
For t = T-1 down to 0: Delta(c_{t-1:0}, c_t) adjusts c_t based on prior load
Compose: C(tau) = c_0 oplus Delta(c_0, c_1) oplus Delta(c_{0:1}, c_2) oplus ...
Apply parallel interference otimes for concurrent operations within each step

algorithms.name: Bottleneck Classification
algorithms.complexity: O(K * |tau|) where K is the number of channels
inputs: Cost tuples C_A, C_B for two UI versions
pseudocode_steps: For each channel i: compute Delta_I_i = I_i(M_B) - I_i(M_A)
For each channel i: compute utilization_i = I_i(M_B) / C_i
If any utilization_i > 1: bottleneck = argmax_i utilization_i
Else if sum utilization_i > sum C_i: bottleneck = cross_channel_interference
Else: bottleneck = max Delta_I_i / C_i
Compute severity = max(0, max utilization_i - 1)

algorithms.name: MaxSMT Repair Synthesis
algorithms.description: Find minimal UI modification that resolves a usability regression using MaxSMT
algorithms.complexity: O(exp(|edits|)) worst case, typically fast for small edit distances
pseudocode_steps: Encode tree T as Z3 variables (position, size, role, children)
Add hard constraints: structural validity, task completability
Add hard constraint: C(T', tau) <= C_max for all tasks tau
For each property p of each node n: add soft constraint (p unchanged)
Invoke Z3 Optimize with maximize(satisfied soft constraints)
Decode Z3 model to get T'
Validate: check C(T') < C(T) and all tasks completable

algorithms.name: Monte Carlo Trajectory Sampling
algorithms.description: Sample trajectories from bounded-rational policy with variance reduction
algorithms.complexity: O(N * T_max * |A|) where N is sample count and T_max is max trajectory length
pseudocode_steps: Initialize RNG with SeedSequence for reproducibility
For i = 1 to N:
s = s_0
For t = 0 to T_max:
Sample a ~ pi_beta(.|s)
Sample s' ~ P(.|s,a)
Record (s, a, c(s,a), t)
If s' in S_G: break
s = s'
Apply variance reduction (control variates, antithetic, stratified)
Compute statistics using Welford's online algorithm

algorithms.name: FDR-Controlled Regression Detection
algorithms.description: Test multiple task specifications with false discovery rate control
algorithms.complexity: O(m * N * T_max) where m is number of tasks
pseudocode_steps: For each task tau_j, j = 1, ..., m:
Sample N trajectories from M_A and M_B under shared bisimulation
Compute paired cost differences Delta_j = C(M_B, tau_j) - C(M_A, tau_j)
Compute p-value p_j using paired t-test (or Wilcoxon)
Sort p-values: p_(1) <= p_(2) <= ... <= p_(m)
Find largest k such that p_(k) <= k * alpha / m (BH threshold)
Reject H_0 for all j with p_(j) <= p_(k)
Report adjusted p-values and bottleneck classifications for rejected tasks

algorithms.name: Channel Capacity via Blahut-Arimoto (Multi-Resource)
algorithms.description: Compute per-resource channel capacities for Wickens MRT model using Blahut-Arimoto with resource-specific channel matrices
algorithms.complexity: O(|R| * |X| * |Y| * N_iter) where |R| is number of resource channels
pseudocode_steps: For each resource r in {visual, auditory, cognitive, motor}:
Run standard Blahut-Arimoto (A2) on P_r to get C_r
For each resource pair (r, r'):
Compute effective capacity C_eff(r) = C_r / (1 + eta_{r,r'} * d_{r'})
Return capacities and water-filling allocation

algorithms.name: Global Sensitivity via Sobol Indices
algorithms.description: Compute first-order and total-effect Sobol sensitivity indices for cognitive cost model parameters using Saltelli sampling
algorithms.complexity: O(N * (2k + 2)) model evaluations where N is sample size, k is number of parameters
inputs: Cognitive cost model f: R^k -> R
outputs: First-order indices S_i with confidence intervals
Total-effect indices S_Ti with confidence intervals
pseudocode_steps: Generate Saltelli quasi-random sample matrices A, B of size N x k
Construct AB_i matrices (A with i-th column from B)
Evaluate f on all 2N + N*k sample points
Compute S_i = Var[E[Y|X_i]] / Var[Y] via Jansen estimator
Compute S_Ti = E[Var[Y|X_{~i}]] / Var[Y]
Bootstrap confidence intervals at level alpha

algorithms.name: Bounded-Rational MCTS Planning
algorithms.description: Monte Carlo Tree Search with softmax action selection reflecting bounded rationality (temperature = 1/beta)
algorithms.complexity: O(N_sim * d * |A| * b) where N_sim is simulation count, d is depth, b is branching factor
inputs: MDP M = (S, A, P, R, gamma)
pseudocode_steps: Initialize root node with state s
Repeat N_sim times:
SELECT: traverse tree using UCB1 with softmax: a = argmax Q(s,a) + c_puct * P(a|s) * sqrt(N(s)) / (1 + N(s,a))
EXPAND: add child node for unexplored action
SIMULATE: rollout using softmax policy with temperature 1/beta
BACKUP: update Q-values and visit counts
Return pi(a|s) proportional to N(s,a)^beta

algorithms.name: Point-Based Value Iteration for POMDP
algorithms.description: Point-based value iteration solving partially observable cognitive task models where UI state is partially hidden
algorithms.complexity: O(|B| * |A| * |O| * |alpha-vectors|) per iteration where |B| is belief point count
inputs: POMDP (S, A, O, P, Z, R, gamma)
outputs: Set of alpha-vectors defining value function
Expected cognitive cost under partial observability
pseudocode_steps: Sample belief points B = {b_1, ..., b_|B|} via exploratory policy
Initialize alpha-vectors from immediate rewards
Repeat until max alpha-vector change < epsilon:
For each belief point b in B:
For each action a: compute backup alpha_a(s) = R(s,a) + gamma * sum_o max_{alpha'} sum_s' P(s'|s,a) Z(o|s',a) alpha'(s')
Select best alpha-vector for b
Prune dominated alpha-vectors
Compute softmax policy over alpha-vector values at temperature 1/beta

algorithms.name: ACT-R Cognitive Simulation
algorithms.description: Simulate user interaction traces using ACT-R architecture with declarative/procedural modules mapped to bounded-rational cost parameters
algorithms.complexity: O(T * |productions| * |chunks|) where T is simulation steps
inputs: ACT-R parameters (latency factor F, noise s, retrieval threshold tau)
outputs: Bounded-rational beta estimate from ACT-R noise
pseudocode_steps: Initialize declarative memory with task-relevant chunks
For each task step t:
Compute chunk activations A_i = B_i + sum_j W_j S_ji + noise(s)
Select production by soft conflict resolution: P(fire_i) = exp(U_i / t_noise) / Z
Execute production: motor/perceptual/cognitive operators
Record latency: T_retrieval = F * exp(-A_max), T_motor from Fitts' law
Map to bounded-rational cost: beta_effective = 1 / t_noise
Return trace and aggregate costs

algorithms.name: WCAG Conformance Evaluation
algorithms.description: Automated WCAG 2.2 conformance evaluation with cognitive cost impact mapping
algorithms.complexity: O(|elements| * |criteria|) where |criteria| is number of WCAG success criteria
outputs: List of WCAG violations with severity
pseudocode_steps: Parse UI into accessibility tree
For each applicable WCAG success criterion:
Run automated check (contrast, keyboard, semantics, ARIA)
If violation found: compute cognitive cost impact via cost model
Aggregate violations by severity and cost impact
Generate prioritized remediation suggestions
Output SARIF 2.1.0 report with cognitive cost annotations

algorithms.name: SARIF-Based Regression Differencing
algorithms.description: Compute usability regression diff between two SARIF reports representing before/after UI states
algorithms.complexity: O(|V_1| * |V_2|) for matching violations across versions
pseudocode_steps: Parse both SARIF reports into violation sets V_A, V_B
Match violations by location, rule ID, and fingerprint
Classify: new = V_B - matched, resolved = V_A - matched, changed = severity differences
Compute cognitive cost delta for each regression
Apply FDR-controlled statistical test on aggregate cost change
Return structured regression report

algorithms.name: Channel Capacity Water-Filling Allocation
algorithms.description: Allocate cognitive resources across MRT channels using water-filling algorithm to maximize total throughput
algorithms.complexity: O(|R| log |R|) for sorting channels, O(|R|) for allocation
pseudocode_steps: Sort channels by noise level N_r / C_r (ascending)
Set water level mu = (P_total + sum(N_r / C_r)) / |R|
For each channel r: P_r = max(0, mu - N_r / C_r)
Adjust for interference: P_r_eff = P_r / (1 + sum_{r'} eta_{r,r'} P_{r'})
Iterate until convergence of effective allocations
Throughput_r = C_r * log(1 + P_r_eff / N_r)

algorithms.name: Rényi DP Budget Tracking
algorithms.description: Track cumulative privacy budget across multiple usability analyses using Rényi divergence-based accounting
algorithms.complexity: O(k * |alpha_grid|) where k is number of composed mechanisms
inputs: Rényi divergence order grid alpha_1, ..., alpha_m
pseudocode_steps: For each mechanism M_i, compute RDP guarantee: epsilon_i(alpha) for each alpha in grid
Compose: epsilon_total(alpha) = sum_i epsilon_i(alpha) (RDP composition theorem)
Convert to (epsilon, delta)-DP: epsilon = min_alpha (epsilon_total(alpha) + log(1/delta) / (alpha - 1))
Track remaining budget: epsilon_remaining = epsilon_target - epsilon
Forecast: estimate mechanisms remaining before budget exhaustion

assumptions.name: Accessibility Tree Well-Formedness
assumptions.statement: The input accessibility tree is a valid rooted tree with unique node identifiers, proper parent-child relationships, and at least one interactive element.

assumptions.name: Task Completability
assumptions.statement: Each task specification defines a reachable goal state from the initial state in both UI versions under consideration.

assumptions.name: Cognitive Parameter Stationarity
assumptions.statement: The cognitive parameters (Fitts' a, b; Hick's a, b; WM capacity; decay rate) are constant within a single comparison, though they may vary between comparisons.

assumptions.name: Channel Independence
assumptions.statement: Cognitive channels (motor, choice, visual, memory) are conditionally independent given the cognitive control state, though they may share resources via the Wickens interference model.

assumptions.name: Prior Policy Regularity
assumptions.statement: The prior policy p_0 assigns positive probability to all actions in all states: p_0(a|s) > 0 for all (s,a).

assumptions.name: Bounded Rewards
assumptions.statement: The reward function is bounded: |R(s,a)| <= R_max for all (s,a), where R_max is a known constant.

assumptions.name: Finite State Space
assumptions.statement: The MDP state space S and action space A are finite. This holds because accessibility trees are finite and the set of possible interactions at any state is bounded.

assumptions.name: Ergodicity
assumptions.statement: Under any bounded-rational policy pi_beta with beta > 0, the induced Markov chain is ergodic (irreducible and aperiodic), ensuring trajectory statistics converge.

assumptions.name: Monotone Regression Signal
assumptions.statement: If UI version B is strictly worse than A on all cognitive dimensions for a task, then the true expected cost satisfies E[C_B] > E[C_A]. The cognitive cost model preserves this ordering.

assumptions.name: Structural Coverage
assumptions.statement: The accessibility tree captures all task-relevant UI structure. Visual properties not reflected in the tree (e.g., CSS styling) are outside the system's scope.

assumptions.name: SMT Solver Completeness
assumptions.statement: The Z3 SMT solver is sound and complete for the quantifier-free theory of linear real arithmetic with uninterpreted functions used in the repair encoding.

assumptions.name: Sample Size Sufficiency
assumptions.statement: The number of Monte Carlo trajectory samples N is sufficient for the central limit theorem to apply: N >= 30 for asymptotic normality of the mean cost estimator.

predictions.expected_outcome: rho >= 0.7 between model cost orderings and human preference orderings on UI pairs
predictions.validation_approach: Compare against published human preference data on UI design variations

predictions.expected_outcome: TPR >= 0.85 for regressions with Cohen's d >= 0.5
predictions.validation_approach: Inject known regressions into real UI pairs and measure detection rate

predictions.expected_outcome: FPR <= alpha (configured significance level) under no-change null
predictions.validation_approach: Run repeated comparisons on identical UI versions

predictions.expected_outcome: >= 0.75 accuracy on expert-labeled bottleneck types
predictions.validation_approach: Expert panel labels bottleneck types on regression cases, compare to system output

predictions.expected_outcome: |S_tilde| / |S| <= 0.1 for production UIs with |S| >= 1000
predictions.validation_approach: Measure quotient size on accessibility trees from 50+ production web applications

predictions.expected_outcome: <= 60 seconds for UIs with <= 500 elements on laptop CPU
predictions.validation_approach: Benchmark on accessibility trees of varying sizes

predictions.expected_outcome: RMSE <= 50ms compared to published Fitts' law data (ISO 9241-9)
predictions.validation_approach: Compare model predictions against ISO 9241-9 throughput datasets

predictions.expected_outcome: RMSE <= 30ms compared to published choice RT data
predictions.validation_approach: Compare against Hick (1952) and Hyman (1953) original datasets

predictions.expected_outcome: C_algebra(tau) / C_simulation(tau) <= 1.5 (50% overestimate at most)
predictions.validation_approach: Compare algebraic cost against discrete-event simulation cost on benchmark tasks

predictions.expected_outcome: Var(Delta C_paired) / Var(Delta C_unpaired) <= 0.3 (70% reduction)
predictions.validation_approach: Measure variance with and without shared bisimulation

predictions.expected_outcome: Repaired UI cost <= 0.9 * regressed UI cost on average
predictions.validation_approach: Apply repairs to detected regressions and measure cost improvement

predictions.expected_outcome: Average edit distance of repairs <= 3 operations
predictions.validation_approach: Count mutations in generated repairs

predictions.expected_outcome: Observed FDR <= 1.1 * nominal alpha across 1000 simulation runs
predictions.validation_approach: Simulation study with known null/alternative task mixtures

predictions.expected_outcome: >= 0.90 recall on WAI-ARIA 1.2 required property/children checks
predictions.validation_approach: Run on W3C ARIA validator test suite

predictions.expected_outcome: >= 0.85 recall on Android Accessibility Scanner known issues
predictions.validation_approach: Compare findings against Google Accessibility Scanner on 20+ apps

predictions.expected_outcome: CV(mean cost) <= 0.05 with N = 1000 samples
predictions.validation_approach: Repeated sampling experiments with known ground truth

predictions.expected_outcome: <= 100 iterations for epsilon = 1e-6 on MDPs with <= 10000 states
predictions.validation_approach: Track convergence on benchmark MDP instances

predictions.metric: Mean absolute error of throughput prediction
predictions.expected_outcome: MAE <= 0.5 bits/s between predicted and observed cognitive throughput on dual-task benchmarks
predictions.validation_approach: Compare Wickens MRT channel capacity predictions against published dual-task performance data

predictions.expected_outcome: Power loss <= 5% for epsilon >= 1.0 with n >= 500 user sessions
predictions.validation_approach: Run DP and non-DP regression tests on synthetic usability datasets and compare power

predictions.metric: Relative error between KLM prediction and bounded-rational cost
predictions.expected_outcome: Relative error <= 15% for beta >= 5.0 on standard KLM benchmark tasks
predictions.validation_approach: Compare KLM predictions against free-energy-optimal policy costs on canonical HCI tasks

predictions.metric: State-space reduction ratio at given distortion
predictions.expected_outcome: Achieves >= 60% state reduction with <= 5% distortion on e-commerce UI models
predictions.validation_approach: Compare rate-distortion quotient size against naive bisimulation on UI MDP benchmarks

predictions.metric: Fraction of state triples satisfying triangle inequality
predictions.expected_outcome: 100% satisfaction (d_cog is a valid pseudometric)
predictions.validation_approach: Exhaustive verification on small MDPs; sampling-based verification on large MDPs

predictions.metric: Coefficient of variation of Sobol indices
predictions.expected_outcome: CV <= 0.05 with N = 10000 * (2k+2) model evaluations for k <= 10 parameters
predictions.validation_approach: Run Sobol analysis on cognitive cost model with known analytical sensitivities

predictions.metric: Recall against manually audited WCAG violations
predictions.expected_outcome: Recall >= 0.80 for Level AA automated-testable success criteria
predictions.validation_approach: Benchmark against W3C WCAG-EM test suite and manual audit results

predictions.claim: Scheduling deadline satisfaction under bounded rationality
predictions.metric: Fraction of tasks completed before deadline
predictions.expected_outcome: Deadline satisfaction >= 85% when total utilization <= 70% of channel capacities
predictions.validation_approach: Simulate multi-task scheduling with varying beta and compare to EDF/RM schedulers

predictions.expected_outcome: Pearson r >= 0.85 on standard ACT-R benchmark tasks
predictions.validation_approach: Compare simulated response times against published ACT-R model validation datasets

predictions.metric: Ratio of actual to predicted composed cost
predictions.expected_outcome: Bound tightness ratio >= 0.75 (actual/upper-bound) on hierarchical task compositions
predictions.validation_approach: Compare operadic cost predictions against Monte Carlo sampled trajectory costs

baselines.name: CogTool
baselines.description: Cognitive modeling tool (John & Salvucci 2005) that predicts task execution time using ACT-R/GOMS models

baselines.name: Additive KLM
baselines.description: Keystroke-Level Model with simple additive cost summation and no interaction terms

baselines.name: WCAG Checker
baselines.description: Static accessibility guideline checker (e.g., axe-core, WAVE) that flags WCAG violations

baselines.name: Screenshot Diff
baselines.description: Visual regression testing tools (Chromatic, Percy) that compare rendered screenshots

baselines.name: LLM Heuristic Evaluation
baselines.description: Large language model prompted to evaluate UI usability heuristics

baselines.name: Random Baseline
baselines.description: Random regression verdict assignment at the configured alpha level
strengths: Controls false positive rate by construction
weaknesses: Zero power for detecting real regressions

baselines.name: Element Count Threshold
baselines.description: Flag regression when the number of interactive elements increases beyond a threshold
weaknesses: Many false positives from benign additions

baselines.name: Tree Edit Distance
baselines.description: Flag regression when the accessibility tree edit distance exceeds a threshold

datasets.name: ARIA Test Suite
datasets.description: W3C WAI-ARIA test cases for conformance validation

datasets.name: Synthetic UI Corpus
datasets.description: Programmatically generated UI pairs with known regressions

datasets.name: Production Web Apps
datasets.description: Accessibility trees from real web applications (open-source)

datasets.name: Android UI Dumps
datasets.description: View hierarchy dumps from popular Android applications

metrics.name: Spearman Rank Correlation
metrics.description: Correlation between model cost orderings and reference orderings

metrics.name: True Positive Rate
metrics.description: Fraction of true regressions correctly detected

metrics.name: False Discovery Rate
metrics.description: Fraction of flagged tasks that are actually non-regressions

metrics.name: Classification Accuracy
metrics.description: Bottleneck type classification accuracy vs expert labels

metrics.name: State Reduction Ratio
metrics.description: Quotient MDP size relative to original MDP size

metrics.name: Repair Effectiveness
metrics.description: Cost reduction achieved by proposed repairs

evaluation_plan.validation_approach: Three-tier validation: (1) Unit validation of cognitive law calibration against published psychophysical data, (2) System validation of regression detection on synthetic UI pairs with known ground truth, (3) Ecological validation of cost orderings against expert judgments on real UI pairs.

packages.core: Foundation types, protocols, configuration, error hierarchy
packages.accessibility: Accessibility tree parsing and representation (HTML, JSON)
packages.aria: W3C ARIA taxonomy, role validation, conformance checking
packages.cognitive: Cognitive law implementations (Fitts', Hick-Hyman, visual search, WM)
packages.variational: Free-energy variational solver, KL divergence, channel capacity
packages.montecarlo: Monte Carlo trajectory sampling, variance reduction, parallel execution
packages.simulation: Discrete-event cognitive simulation (GOMS, KLM, ACT-R activation)
packages.algebra: Compositional cognitive cost algebra (sequential, parallel, context)
packages.comparison: Statistical comparison of cost distributions across UI versions
packages.statistics: Hypothesis testing, FDR control, effect size, power analysis, bootstrap
packages.smt_repair: Z3-backed SMT repair synthesis, constraint generation
packages.interval: Interval arithmetic, affine arithmetic, IEEE 1788 compliance
packages.output: Formatting (JSON, SARIF, HTML, console, GitHub integration)
packages.information_theory: Deep information-theoretic primitives (entropy, mutual information, channel capacity, rate-distortion, free energy, bounds estimation)
packages.channel: Wickens Multiple Resource Theory channel capacity modeling, interference computation, water-filling allocation, temporal scheduling
packages.scheduling: Task scheduling under bounded rationality with deadline pressure, priority computation, multitask optimization, schedulability analysis
packages.sensitivity: Parametric sensitivity analysis (Sobol indices, Morris screening, local gradients, variance decomposition, robustness certification, calibration)
packages.differential: Differential privacy mechanisms for usability data (Laplace, Gaussian, exponential, RDP/zCDP accounting, local DP, private aggregation)
packages.wcag: WCAG 2.2 conformance checking (contrast, keyboard navigation, semantic analysis, ARIA validation, cognitive cost mapping, remediation reporting)
packages.goms: GOMS/KLM-GOMS cognitive architecture (KLM prediction, task decomposition, GOMS simulation, critical path analysis, optimization)
